title: Wire firmware time build epoch to SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH metadata
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-2067 implements the firmware time provider with a deterministic Kconfig build epoch floor, but docs/firmware-time-provider.md also requires production builds without deterministic build epoch metadata to fail and developer generated timestamps to be explicit/tested. What needs to be done: integrate the Zephyr/LICHEN build flow so production firmware derives CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX from SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH or an explicit release epoch, fails production builds when the epoch is missing, and has native_sim/Twister coverage for missing production metadata and explicit non-reproducible developer timestamp mode. No hardware required.
notes:
User clarified that build-time stamps in firmware or board metadata can be used to epoch GNSS/date-time samples. Follow-up should make that source explicit in the build flow: deterministic SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH or release epoch for production, optional explicit developer timestamp mode, and native_sim/Twister coverage for missing/invalid metadata.
Implementation completed for no-hardware build epoch metadata wiring. Added lichen/cmake/lichen_build_epoch.cmake to resolve CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX before Zephyr Kconfig loads. Production mode now requires SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH or -DLICHEN_RELEASE_EPOCH_UNIX; SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH takes precedence if both are present. Developer mode is explicit via -DLICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_MODE=developer-generated, with distinct source metadata for host-generated DEVELOPER_GENERATED vs fixed developer override DEVELOPER_FIXED. Wired production firmware entrypoints lichen/apps/gateway, lichen/apps/puck, and firmware/bridge-zephyr to call the helper before find_package(Zephyr). Added matching Kconfig source symbols in modern HAL and bridge-local legacy Kconfig. Added lichen/tests/build_epoch Twister coverage for SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, release epoch, developer-generated, and developer-fixed metadata. Added Python CMake policy tests for missing production metadata hard-fail, all firmware entrypoint early failures, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH precedence, generated developer timestamp path, and developer-fixed source distinction.

Review evidence: initial reviews found P1 SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH precedence inversion, P1 weak missing-production Twister coverage, P2 developer-generated path not exercised, P2 developer fixed/generated ambiguity, and P1 bridge-zephyr entrypoint bypass. Fixed all. Final reviews Gauss and Copernicus found no remaining P0/P1/P2 issues; Gauss verified bridge helper call before find_package and bridge Kconfig symbols; Copernicus verified precedence, entrypoint coverage, developer-generated/fixed distinction, and bridge follow-up. Third final review pending/record separately before close.

Validation evidence: local git diff --check passed; python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py passed 5/5; cd python && python3 -m ruff check tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py passed. EC2 i-04d7dc29208da05dd in us-east-2a using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-build-epoch: bootstrap-host hit known Ubuntu Noble awscli apt issue, installed minimal build packages manually. Twister command '. /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh && cd /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-build-epoch && west twister -T lichen/tests/build_epoch -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out/build_epoch --inline-logs -x ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen' passed 8/8 configs, 8 cases, 0 warnings. Regression command 'west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/app_interface -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out/time_provider_regression --inline-logs --integration -x ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen' passed 7/8 configs, 1 static skip, 150 cases, 36 skipped, 0 warnings. Gateway missing metadata command failed at configure with expected requires SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH error. Gateway SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH+release command built qemu_x86 and .config showed CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1715555555 with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH source, proving precedence. Gateway release-only qemu_x86 build passed and .config showed CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1716666666 with RELEASE source; RAM 246716 B / 31 MB (0.76%), IDT 0/2 KB. Bridge missing metadata failed at configure with expected error. Bridge SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH configure succeeded and .config showed CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1717777777 with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH source, but full bridge qemu_x86 build failed on pre-existing duplicate-symbol linkage between bridge vendored LICHEN sources and the modern module; filed project-LICHEN-2067.1.1 with exact evidence. Known qemu warnings: CONFIG_TIMER_RANDOM_GENERATOR test-only and no sources for some driver libraries.
Final third review: Lagrange reviewed the current uncommitted build-epoch patch after all fixes and found no remaining P0/P1/P2 issues. Lightweight checks run by reviewer: pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py passed 5/5; python3 -m ruff check python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py passed; git diff --check passed. Reviewer noted project-LICHEN-2067.1.1 as the only known bridge duplicate-symbol blocker and did not count it as a new finding for this patch.
EC2 cleanup completed after closure: ran sync and sudo umount /mnt/lichen-zephyr on i-04d7dc29208da05dd, detached EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and verified state=available, terminated instance i-04d7dc29208da05dd and verified state=terminated, deleted temporary security group sg-086ff51cb4e6497a2 and verified describe-security-groups returns InvalidGroup.NotFound.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware build epoch metadata wiring: production firmware entrypoints derive CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX from SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH or explicit release epoch, fail without metadata, distinguish developer-generated vs developer-fixed metadata, and have Python plus native_sim/qemu Twister validation. Bridge full build duplicate-symbol blocker filed as project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.
